title: Conditions for a Hindu marriage
text: A marriage may be solemnized between any two Hindus, if the following conditions are fulfilled: neither party has a spouse living; neither party is incapable of giving valid consent; neither party is suffering from mental disorder; the parties are not within prohibited degrees of relationship; the parties are not sapindas of each other.

title: Restitution of conjugal rights
text: When either the husband or the wife has, without reasonable excuse, withdrawn from the society of the other, the aggrieved party may apply for restitution of conjugal rights.

title: Judicial separation
text: Either party may seek judicial separation on grounds specified in Section 13. The marriage remains legally valid, but parties are not obliged to cohabit.

title: Grounds for Divorce
text: A divorce may be granted on any of the following grounds: adultery; cruelty; desertion for two years or more; conversion to another religion; incurable unsoundness of mind; virulent and incurable leprosy; venereal disease; renunciation of the world; not heard alive for seven years. Additionally, a wife may seek divorce if husband has married again, committed rape, sodomy or bestiality, or failed to comply with maintenance orders.

title: Divorce by Mutual Consent
text: Both parties may jointly file a petition for divorce by mutual consent if they have lived separately for one year or more and agree that the marriage has broken down irretrievably.

title: No petition for divorce within one year of marriage
text: No petition for divorce shall be presented within one year of marriage unless the case involves exceptional hardship or depravity.

title: Divorced person when may marry again
text: When a marriage has been dissolved and there is no right of appeal, or the appeal period has expired without an appeal being filed, or an appeal has been dismissed, either party may marry again.

title: Legitimacy of children of void and voidable marriages
text: Children born of void or voidable marriages are deemed legitimate, but such legitimacy does not confer inheritance rights on property not belonging to the parents.

title: Maintenance pendente lite and expenses of proceedings
text: Either the wife or the husband may claim maintenance and expenses during the pendency of proceedings under this Act. The court may order the other party to pay maintenance, considering the income and financial capacity of both parties.

title: Permanent alimony and maintenance
text: The court may, upon passing any decree under this Act, order the respondent to pay permanent alimony and maintenance to the applicant for life or for a specified period, considering the income and property of both parties and their conduct.

title: Custody of children
text: In any proceeding under this Act, the court may pass interim or final orders regarding custody, maintenance and education of minor children, considering their welfare.

title: Disposal of property
text: The court may make provisions regarding the disposal of property jointly owned by both parties at the time of passing any decree under this Act.

title: Appeals from decrees and orders
text: All decrees and certain orders under this Act are appealable to the appropriate appellate court.

title: Void marriages
text: Any marriage solemnized after the commencement of this Act shall be null and void if it contravenes any one of the conditions specified in clauses (i), (iv), and (v) of Section 5.

title: Voidable marriages
text: A marriage shall be voidable and may be annulled by a decree if: the marriage has not been consummated due to the impotence of the respondent; consent was obtained by force or fraud; or at the time of marriage, the respondent was pregnant by another person.

title: Court to which petition shall be presented
text: Every petition under this Act shall be presented to the District Court within the jurisdiction where: the marriage was solemnized; the respondent resides; or where the parties last resided together.

title: Application of Civil Procedure Code
text: Subject to the provisions contained in this Act, all proceedings under this Act shall be regulated, as far as may be, by the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

title: Decree in proceedings
text: In any proceeding under this Act, the court shall satisfy itself that there is no collusion between the parties, and that the petitioner is not taking advantage of his or her own wrong, before granting any relief.
